organization.cf_accounts_flowchart:
1. Decide wither prospect is a valid customer. follow the flow chart below.



 
  1. Evaluate customer size
    • What is the average amount of monthly tests by segment
      • Small and niche lab focusing on certain tests like hormones averages at 500-3000 tests (Type c)
      • Small independent lab/community lab averages at 2000-10,000 tests (Type B)
      • Medium size labs average at 10,000-50,000 tests (Type A)
      • Large size labs average at 50,000-250,000 tests (STAR Type)
 
  1. Assess logistics and ordering cadence
    • Where should we deliver- Single location or multiple?
    • Do they have their own carrier account we can use?
    • How frequent do you issue orders? (Weekly/Bi-Weekly/Monthly, Random)
    • Who usually issue orders? (lab manager/back-office/other)
    • Who approves quotes and prices?
 
  1. Gauge for areas of weakness where we can show added value
    • Do you have good visibility to your orders and quotes (status, lead-time, TN, invoices, etc.)
      • We can enable customer portal with all of the above including automated scheduled reports
    • Do you have direct and accessible channel to get your questions and issues answered and resolved?
      • Emphasis your direct and accessible communication approach
    • Would you appreciate support in converting test demand into material orders?
      • We have an easy to use tool to convert tests demand into products (reagents) orders
    • What are the payment terms you have today? Does it support your operation?
      • We can offer CC or Net30
    • Would you find value in JIT4You holding critical items in our inventory, available for a next day delivery?
      • We can hold certain items on allocation for fast deliveries
